summary: Phase 5 (Admin CMS, Services, Directories, Global Search, Track) tested end-to-end. Backend: 29/30 pytest pass (1 fail surfaced a real backend bug). Frontend: all Phase 5 UI flows work — admin login, 8 stat cards, all 6 CMS collections with correct counts (countries 5 / products 5 / corridors 4 / hsn_codes 5 / industries 8 / blog 6), JSON edit modal, Leads search+CSV, Service Requests rows + status/CA controls, Events tab shows captured test_event, /services lists 10 cards across Govt Documentation + Consulting, /services/iec-registration FAQ accordion (3 items) + lead form submit shows success, /directory has 5 category cards, /directory/exporters shows 6 cards, /search 'rice' returns results, Nav exposes Services and Directories. NOTE: Home (/) throws an uncaught runtime error 'globeRef.current.globeMaterial is not a function' — regression in the hero Globe component (Phase 1 code).

minor.issue: Type field uses naive 'rstrip("s")' which yields 'countrie' and 'industrie' instead of 'country' / 'industry'. Spec (and UI badge) expects singular forms. Products/corridors are fine.

ui_bugs.issue: Uncaught TypeError: globeRef.current.globeMaterial is not a function shown as full-screen dev overlay on /. Reproduces on every load.
Content tab uses key 'cms' (data-testid=admin-tab-cms) but Phase 5 spec listed it as admin-tab-content. Minor naming mismatch; functionally fine.
Spec listed testids 'login-submit'/'login-error' but code uses 'admin-login-submit'/'admin-login-error'. Minor naming mismatch.

action_items: Fix Home page Globe runtime error: globeRef.current.globeMaterial is not a function — likely globe.gl API change or null-ref on hot reload. This blocks the landing page with a red error overlay.
Fix /api/global-search type singularization. Replace 'collection_name.rstrip("s")' with explicit mapping {countries: country, industries: industry, products: product, corridors: corridor}.
Optional: align testids in AdminDashboard.jsx with Phase 5 spec ('admin-tab-content' instead of 'admin-tab-cms', 'login-submit' instead of 'admin-login-submit') OR update spec — but UI works as-is.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1725 lines — strongly recommended to split into routers (admin.py, services.py, directory.py, search.py, track.py) before Phase 6.
All Phase 5 data (SERVICES_DB, DIRECTORY_DB) remains MOCKED in-memory; CMS collections are seeded to MongoDB only if empty (idempotent). This is intentional per spec.
Global search _scan() depends on cms_* collections being seeded; works once startup seed runs. Falls back silently on exception which can hide real DB errors — consider logging.
Service-request endpoint creates BOTH a service_request and a separate lead doc — verified working but the two are not linked by id. Consider adding lead.serviceRequestId for traceability.
Globe component crash on Home is a Phase 1 regression that should be triaged before Phase 6.

success_rate.frontend: 94% (Phase 5 fully working; Home globe regression unrelated to Phase 5)

test_credentials: Admin token: leadnation-admin-2026 (X-Admin-Token header; query token for /admin/leads.csv).
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_Phase5_Product (CRUD), TEST_Phase5_SR service request + linked lead, TEST_UI_SR via /services/iec-registration form, and test_event_phase5 analytics events. All prefixed TEST_ for easy cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 5 endpoints verified at /app/backend/tests/test_phase5_admin.py — 30 tests covering admin auth, CMS CRUD, leads CSV, services, service-requests (+lead creation + assign CA), 5 directory kinds (exporters=6), global-search, /track + /admin/events. The only backend failure is TestGlobalSearch.test_india which exposes the rstrip('s') bug — kept failing intentionally. Phase 1-4 regression NOT re-run this iteration; previous iteration_3.json reported 60/60. Frontend: admin tab data-testid keys are 'dashboard|cms|leads|service-requests|events' (NOT 'content'). Globe error on Home requires Phase 1 fix.
rca of the issue: GLOBAL-SEARCH BUG: server.py line ~1645 emits `collection_name.rstrip('s')` as the type. 'countries'->'countrie', 'industries'->'industrie'. Fix: replace with explicit map. HOME GLOBE ERROR: globe.gl threw 'globeMaterial is not a function' — most likely the React component calls globeRef.current.globeMaterial() before scene is ready or against an unsupported version. Reproduce: navigate to /. Mitigation: guard with `typeof globeMaterial === 'function'` or upgrade/downgrade react-globe.gl.
